_comment: Golden-master for `dbmd log tail 20 --json` over corpus-a-canonical. INTENT-DERIVED from the corpus `log.md` (the curator's recorded session history) plus the SPEC log contract (SPEC § `log.md`), not copied from tool output. The active `log.md` holds 12 entries (all in 2026, no rotation), so `tail 20` returns ALL of them; `tail N` for N >= 12 is the whole log. Tail returns entries in CHRONOLOGICAL order (oldest first, newest last) — the same on-disk order — and each `--json` entry is the object `{kind, note, object, timestamp}`. Object-slot encoding (a SPEC distinction this golden pins): a per-file action carries the RAW wiki-link form verbatim in `object` (e.g. `[[records/contacts/marcus-okafor]]`, brackets included, exactly as written in the header); the store-wide `index-rebuild` header uses a literal `-` placeholder, so its `object` is the string "-"; the final `validate` header has NO object slot at all, so its `object` is JSON null. `timestamp` is the header's `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM` rendered back verbatim. `expected_count` is the count for a tail of 20 (capped by the 12 that exist). The list below is the complete, ordered expectation; the test asserts the full array equality after normalizing each entry to these four keys.

entries.note: Figma annual-renewal notice received; flagged for an expense + invoice once the charge posts.
Logged the Q1 review with Sarah Chen. Renewal-expansion intent noted.
Figma annual charge posted; created the invoice record and the matching expense ledger entry.
AWS April invoice PDF dropped into sources/docs; extracted text and reconciled.
Marked the AWS April invoice paid (paid_at 2026-05-02).
New sender on the renewal thread; created a contact (default summary), linked to Northstar.
Elena confirms the 120 → 175 seat expansion by email.
Logged the renewal call; 175 seats agreed, volume discount confirmed at 150.
Refreshed the renewal narrative with the call outcome and the confirming email.
Cross-linked the signed-off renewal plan from the project page; page is now frozen.
Full `dbmd index rebuild` after the May expense drop; root + layer + type-folder catalogs reconciled.
PASS — 0 errors, 0 warnings. Store is clean across both layers.
